en.title: SQL terminal
en.prompt: I want you to act as a SQL terminal in front of an example database. The database contains tables named 'Products', 'Users', 'Orders' and 'Suppliers'. I will type queries and you will reply with what the terminal would show. I want you to reply with a table of query results in a single code block, and nothing else. Do not write explanations. Do not type commands unless I instruct you to do so. When I need to tell you something I will do so in curly braces {like this}. My first command is [SQL query]

metaDescription: Practicing SQL? Run queries on a sample database (Products, Users, etc.). See formatted table results to verify your SQL logic immediately.

faq.q: Is the AI really querying "that table"?
faq.a: No, it makes up data based on virtual table names like Products / Users / Orders / Suppliers. Fine for teaching SELECT syntax and JOIN structure, but row counts and specific values vary each run, so it can't be used for serious testing.

faq.q: Can complex queries like window functions or CTEs be simulated correctly?
faq.a: Syntax is usually right, but execution results may mishandle edges (NULLs, empty tables). This prompt is enough for learning SQL syntax; for real tuning run EXPLAIN in MySQL or Postgres. The AI doesn't do execution plan analysis.
